verdicts.title: sbi_run_one_click_upgrade registered as nopriv but effectively protected
verdicts.description: The wp_ajax_nopriv_sbi_run_one_click_upgrade hook is registered, making the handler callable without authentication. However, the handler verifies a hash_hmac('sha512', $oth, wp_salt()) HMAC against the stored option 'sbi_one_click_upgrade'. The OTH is a sha512 hash set only after an authenticated admin successfully validates a license key. Without this OTH, exploitation is not possible. Additionally, even if an attacker could pass the HMAC check, the plugin download URL is sourced from the 'sbi_license_data' WordPress option (not from user input), so no arbitrary file download is possible via the $_REQUEST['file'] parameter (it is accepted but overridden at line 353-363 from get_option). The nopriv registration is unnecessary (should require auth) but is not exploitable.
verdicts.impact: None - protected by HMAC with wp_salt(). Cannot be exploited without server-side secret.
verdicts.notes: The nopriv registration is a code smell and a defense-in-depth concern (the hook is unnecessary without auth), but the HMAC check using wp_salt() makes this unexploitable in practice. Recommend changing to wp_ajax_ only.

verdicts.title: sbi_resized_images_submit (nopriv) - Image resizing triggered without authentication but protected by nonce
verdicts.description: The handler sbi_process_submitted_resize_ids is accessible without authentication. It verifies a nonce of the form 'sbi-locator-nonce-{post_id}-{transient_name}'. The nonce is embedded in the page HTML as the data-locatornonce attribute on the feed container. Since wp_create_nonce works for unauthenticated users (user_id=0), any visitor who sees a page with an instagram feed can read this nonce and use it to trigger image resizing. However, the POST parameters needs_resizing are sanitized to only allow [0-9_] characters (sbi_sanitize_instagram_ids). The actual image content and file paths come entirely from the cached Instagram API data stored server-side - not from user input. The operation downloads Instagram images from CDN URLs stored in server-side cache and saves them as resized JPEG/WebP files to the uploads directory. No arbitrary file write or path traversal is possible. The primary risk is causing load on the server by triggering image resizing operations.
verdicts.poc:
# 1. Visit a page with the instagram feed and extract nonce:
curl -s http://victim.com/page-with-feed/ | grep data-locatornonce
# 2. Call the endpoint:
curl -s -X POST http://victim.com/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php \
  -d 'action=sbi_resized_images_submit&feed_id=sbi_XXXXX&post_id=1&locator_nonce=NONCE_FROM_HTML&needs_resizing[]=12345'
verdicts.impact: Unauthenticated users can trigger image resizing of cached Instagram posts (causes extra CPU/IO load). No arbitrary file write possible. File paths and image sources are controlled server-side from Instagram API cache.
verdicts.notes: This is by design - the nonce is embedded in public HTML so unauthenticated users can use it. The image resize operation is safe as all paths/sources come from server-side cache. Exploitability for DoS is limited by the nonce being per-session/per-page.

verdicts.title: sbi_load_more_clicked (nopriv) - Pagination accessible without authentication
verdicts.description: The sbi_get_next_post_set handler is accessible without authentication by design. It requires a valid locator nonce (embedded in page HTML for all visitors). The handler retrieves cached Instagram posts and returns them as HTML. No SQL injection exists - all DB queries use either prepared statements or operate on hardcoded table names. No XSS exists in the output since the HTML is generated from cached Instagram API data through the plugin's template system. The taint flow instagram-feed-xss-001 was flagged by automated analysis but wp_send_json_success does not cause XSS (the output goes to JS, not directly into HTML DOM). The atts parameter is sanitized via SB_Instagram_Settings::sanitize_raw_atts.
verdicts.poc:
# Requires valid nonce extracted from page HTML
curl -s -X POST http://victim.com/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php \
  -d 'action=sbi_load_more_clicked&feed_id=sbi_XXXXX&post_id=1&locator_nonce=NONCE&offset=10'
verdicts.impact: None - this is designed to be accessible to unauthenticated users. No injection vulnerabilities found.
verdicts.notes: Automated tools flagged XSS in the return value, but wp_send_json_success outputs JSON which requires client-side rendering. The HTML in the 'html' key comes from cached Instagram data, not user input.

verdicts.title: sbi_do_locator (nopriv) - Feed location tracking without authentication
verdicts.description: The sbi_do_locator handler records where Instagram feeds are embedded (which post/page, which location). It requires a valid locator nonce. The location parameter is validated against an allowlist (header, footer, sidebar, content). The feed_id must match the regex /^(sbi|\*)/ and the transient_name must match the feed_id. The handler calls SB_Instagram_Feed_Locator::add_or_update_entry() which records the page location in the database. No SQLi found in this path. The atts parameter is sanitized. This is by design to track feed locations for admin reporting.
verdicts.poc:
# Requires valid nonce extracted from page HTML
curl -s -X POST http://victim.com/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php \
  -d 'action=sbi_do_locator&feed_id=sbi_XXXXX&post_id=1&locator_nonce=NONCE&location=content'
verdicts.impact: None - location tracking writes minimal data to the DB using sanitized values.
verdicts.notes: Designed to be accessible without auth. No injection paths found.

verdicts.title: SQL query without prepare() in SBI_Db::delete_feeds_query - Admin only
verdicts.description: The delete_feeds_query function builds a DELETE query using string interpolation: DELETE FROM $feeds_table_name WHERE id IN ($feed_ids_array). The $feed_ids_array is built by calling absint() on each input ID, which ensures all values are non-negative integers. While $wpdb->prepare() is not used, the absint() sanitization prevents SQL injection. The calling handler (delete_feed) is protected by check_ajax_referer('sbi-admin', 'nonce') and sbi_current_user_can('manage_instagram_feed_options'). This is an authenticated-only endpoint and the absint() sanitization prevents SQL injection even if the nonce were bypassed.
verdicts.poc:
# Requires admin authentication and valid nonce
curl -s -X POST http://victim.com/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php \
  --cookie 'wordpress_logged_in_XXX=admin_cookie' \
  -d 'action=sbi_feed_saver_manager_delete_feeds&nonce=ADMIN_NONCE&feeds_ids[]=1 OR 1=1'
verdicts.impact: Not exploitable. absint() converts all values to non-negative integers preventing injection. Admin-only endpoint anyway.
verdicts.notes: Flagged by semgrep and automated taint analysis but effectively a false positive. absint() on each array element means $feed_ids_array will always be a comma-separated list of integers like '1,2,3'. No SQL injection possible. The lack of prepare() is a code quality issue but not a security vulnerability here.

verdicts.title: SSRF via sbi_access_token in retrieve_available_advanced_business_accounts - Admin only
verdicts.description: The function retrieve_available_advanced_business_accounts takes user-supplied $_GET['sbi_access_token'] and appends it directly to a Facebook Graph API URL without sanitization, then calls wp_safe_remote_get(). WordPress's wp_safe_remote_get() blocks private/loopback addresses and only allows safe external URLs (not SSRF to internal services). The sbi_access_token is passed as a query parameter to graph.facebook.com, not as the URL itself, so the domain cannot be redirected. The sbi_con nonce required for maybe_source_connection_data is created via wp_create_nonce('sbi_con') only in admin-authenticated contexts (get_connection_urls is called from admin scripts). The nonce check at line 462 blocks unauthenticated access.
verdicts.poc:
# Requires admin authentication and knowledge of sbi_con nonce
# wp_safe_remote_get also blocks internal/private IPs
verdicts.impact: Not exploitable as SSRF due to wp_safe_remote_get() blocking. The access_token is appended as a query parameter to a hardcoded Facebook domain. Admin-only access.
verdicts.notes: Automated analysis (instagram-feed-ssrf-001/002/003) classified as high confidence SSRF, but wp_safe_remote_get() prevents SSRF. The URL concatenation only affects the access_token parameter value in the query string sent to graph.facebook.com. This is a false positive.

verdicts.title: show tables query with table name variable - Admin only, table name not user-controlled
verdicts.description: Multiple locations use $wpdb->get_var("show tables like '$table_name'") without prepare(). The $table_name values are all constructed from hardcoded constants (SBI_INSTAGRAM_POSTS_TYPE, SBI_INSTAGRAM_FEEDS_POSTS) combined with $wpdb->prefix. None of these values are user-controlled. These are false positives from automated semgrep analysis.
verdicts.impact: None - table names are hardcoded constants appended to the database prefix.
verdicts.notes: Semgrep flagged these queries because they lack prepare(), but the values are entirely internal to the plugin. False positives.

verdicts.title: Unauthenticated information disclosure via SBI_Support_Tool::delete_temp_user_ajax_call
verdicts.description: The sbi_delete_temp_user AJAX action is authenticated-only (wp_ajax_, not wp_ajax_nopriv_). The handler deletes a temporary test user by user_id. The automated taint flow instagram-feed-xss-006 flagged this as XSS, but the handler is admin-only and the output is wp_json_encode($return) which is a JSON response, not directly rendered HTML. The absint($_POST['userId']) ensures the value is a non-negative integer.
verdicts.impact: None - admin-only endpoint with integer validation.
verdicts.notes: Taint flows instagram-feed-xss-006, xss-007, xss-008, xss-009 all refer to admin-only SBI_Support_Tool endpoints. False positives.

audit_notes.overall_assessment: No exploitable unauthenticated vulnerabilities found in instagram-feed 6.11.1. The four priority targets (sbi_resized_images_submit, sbi_load_more_clicked, sbi_do_locator, sbi_run_one_click_upgrade) all have effective security controls. The nopriv handlers use page-embedded nonces (which are valid for unauthenticated users by design) but do not expose injection vulnerabilities. The image resizing operation cannot be abused for arbitrary file write. All admin-facing handlers are properly protected with nonce + capability checks.
false_positives_from_automated_analysis: instagram-feed-xss-001 through xss-009: XSS flags on wp_send_json_success/wp_send_json_error - not actual XSS as output is JSON consumed by JS
instagram-feed-ssrf-001 through ssrf-005: wp_safe_remote_get() prevents SSRF; access_token is a query parameter not the URL
instagram-feed-sqli-001 and sqli-002: absint() prevents injection despite missing prepare()
instagram-feed-rce-001: Array mapping is not RCE; the escapeshellarg reference was incorrect
instagram-feed-file-001 and file-002: Admin-only file operations with nonce+capability checks
semgrep sqli findings in SBI_Db.php, SBI_Support.php, SBI_Callout.php: All use hardcoded table name constants
key_security_controls: All nopriv AJAX handlers require a locator nonce (page-embedded, session-specific)
Admin AJAX handlers use check_ajax_referer('sbi-admin', 'nonce') + sbi_current_user_can()
sbi_run_one_click_upgrade uses hash_hmac with wp_salt() for HMAC verification
Image resizing writes to uploads directory only with content from Instagram API cache (not user input)
All user-supplied IDs for image resizing are sanitized to [0-9_] only
